summary: Clean credential storage implementation with good test coverage of happy/unhappy paths. A few type safety and test precision issues worth addressing.

suggestions.description: Type assertion `content as string` violates project guidelines
suggestions.rationale: Project CLAUDE.md states: No any types or type assertions. This `as string` cast bypasses type checking on the writeFileSync mock call argument.
suggestions.suggestion: Use a runtime check instead: `expect(typeof content).toBe("string")` followed by using the value, or narrow via `String(content)`.

suggestions.description: Type assertion `value as Record<string, unknown>` in type guard
suggestions.rationale: While common in type guards, `as` casts are explicitly forbidden by project guidelines. This one is low-risk since the null/object check precedes it, but it still violates the stated rule.
suggestions.suggestion: Use Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty or `in` operator checks directly on `value` after the object check, e.g. `"token" in value && typeof (value as any).token` -- or accept this as a pragmatic exception and document it.

suggestions.description: existsSync mock returns false for ALL calls, not just the directory check
suggestions.rationale: The mock does not distinguish between paths. If saveCredentials ever adds a second existsSync call (e.g., checking if the file already exists before writing), this test would silently break or pass for the wrong reason.
suggestions.suggestion: Use mockImplementation with a function that checks the argument: `vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockImplementation((p) => p \!== CREDENTIALS_DIR)` or use mockReturnValueOnce.

suggestions.description: No test asserts mkdirSync is NOT called when directory already exists
suggestions.rationale: The writes-credentials test sets existsSync to true but never asserts mkdirSync was not called. This leaves the skip-mkdir path unverified.

suggestions.description: No boundary test for expiration at exactly the current time
suggestions.rationale: Implementation uses `<=` so credentials expiring at exactly Date.now() are treated as expired. This is a reasonable choice but the boundary is untested.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test using vi.useFakeTimers to set a fixed Date.now(), then create credentials with expiresAt equal to that exact time, and assert null is returned.
